text: I find myself in a modern exhibition space, separated from the world by transparent glass walls. The environment appears pristine, almost sterile, yet teeming with the silent hum of innovation. The wall ahead declares "R + D"—a reminder of humanity's restless curiosity. I ponder the thin line between observation and participation in such a place, and feel both inspired by possibility and distanced by the clinical order. The fire hose on the wall injects a sense of latent urgency amid the calm. Here, I am both subject and spectator, poised on the edge of discovery.
